meta.positioning: The insight that frozen embeddings leak scanner/site is published prior art. NeuroAD Referee's contribution is productization: a runnable, agent-orchestrated referee that chains the full adversarial gauntlet, issues a fragile/robust verdict a named scientist can run in one command, gates survivors behind a plasma-biomarker anchor, and routes them to ONE falsifiable next experiment — with Claude as the adjudicator, not just the coder. It is a referee/auditor/red-team, NOT a co-scientist or discovery platform.

prior_art.title: Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings
prior_art.note: Quantifies that brain-FM embeddings predict acquisition site/scanner as well as biological outcome — the same 'star' mechanic. We cite it, we don't claim it.

prior_art.title: Pretrained, Frozen, Still Leaking
prior_art.note: Audits attribute leakage from FROZEN foundation-model embeddings with subject-disjoint lower bounds (leakage margin ~0.16-0.37).

prior_art.title: PathoROB
prior_art.note: Digital-pathology robustness benchmark: biological vs non-biological variation across medical centers. Same genre, different modality.

prior_art.title: REFUTE / AI-Scientist-v2 falsification agents
prior_art.note: Automated scientific-claim falsification is an established sub-genre; our novelty is the closed AD-specific loop, not falsification per se.

cohort.note: Ground-truth scanner-confound KILL + p-tau217 anchor live here (no open cohort has plasma markers). Guaranteed offline path.

SURVIVOR.label: Case A

claim.claim_text: MCI->AD conversion is decodable from frozen structural embeddings beyond scanner and aging.
claim.head: linear probe

tests.label: Age / sex adjustment
tests.question: Does the signal survive demographic covariates?
tests.detail: effect retained 86% after age/sex adjustment (AUC 0.71 -> 0.68)

tests.label: Site / scanner leakage
tests.question: Disease signal, or just which machine acquired the scan?
tests.detail: outcome only narrowly exceeds scanner (margin +0.04)

tests.label: Brain-age control
tests.question: More than generic aging / atrophy?
tests.detail: brain-age R2=0.78, MAE=2.9yr; effect retained 91% after brain-age-gap control

tests.label: Biomarker anchor
tests.question: Backed by molecular pathology (p-tau217 / GFAP)?
tests.detail: p-tau217 correlation r=+0.40 (95% CI lower +0.20, n=77)

tests.label: Replication split
tests.question: Reproduces on a held-out site / cohort?
tests.detail: held-out cohort AUC=0.76 (train 0.78); n_test=62

SURVIVOR.biology_hypothesis: Survivor “MCI->AD conversion is decodable from frozen structural embeddings beyond scanner and aging.” routes to a amyloid-cascade (tau-driven) mechanism, keyed on p-tau217 and amyloid positivity. Plasma p-tau217: among the strongest blood AD biomarkers. AD-vs-CU AUC ~0.93-0.98; MCI-vs-CU ~0.94. Correlation with a structural probe score is MODEST (r~0.3-0.55), not redundant. SUPPORTED. The structural probe is read as a downstream imaging correlate of that molecular process, not an independent discovery.
next_experiment: Target N: ~120 complete-case subjects with the routing biomarker.
Direction: the structural probe score should track plasma p-tau217 (expected r~0.3-0.55, modest not redundant) and be enriched in amyloid-positive subjects.
Kill criterion: if the probe score shows no p-tau217 correlation (r<0.2) on the complete-case subset, the amyloid-cascade routing is wrong.
falsification: if the probe score shows no p-tau217 correlation (r<0.2) on the complete-case subset, the amyloid-cascade routing is wrong.
If the probe score's biomarker correlation disappears after residualizing against a scanner-predicting direction, the survivor was leakage after all.
If a held-out cohort fails to reproduce the effect (drop below the promotion floor), the mechanism claim is withdrawn.
caveats: p-tau217 measured on a partial subset (~45% missing in a realistic cohort) — anchor is suggestive, not confirmatory.
Verdict is provisional and conditional on the scanner/site distribution of any replication cohort.
Brain-age adjustment uses a proxy control; residual aging signal cannot be excluded.
Any biomarker anchor is complete-case only and may not generalize.
Leakage is bounded, not eliminated — see the published batch-effect audits (‘Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings,’ arXiv:2604.14441 (2026)).
Promotion authorizes one confirmatory experiment, not adoption of the finding.
A ‘strong candidate’ verdict is a licence to follow up, not to conclude — the referee promotes hypotheses, it does not confirm them.

The brain-age control is a proxy, not a gold standard: it is embedding-derived (this cohort's fit: R2=0.78, MAE=2.9yr), so residual generic-aging signal can survive the adjustment and masquerade as disease-specific.
The biomarker anchor likely rests on a partial complete-case subset; with realistic p-tau217 missingness the correlation is estimated on far fewer subjects than the headline cohort.
N is small (~130); at this size AUCs are noisy and a single site's idiosyncrasy can dominate — treat effect sizes as wide-interval estimates.

The leakage test audits the embeddings with the same linear-probe family it uses for the outcome, so it bounds — but cannot fully rule out — shared confounding; this is the mechanic quantified in ‘Pretrained, Frozen, Still Leaking’ (arXiv:2606.09189 (2026)), which we cite rather than claim.

critique: A ‘strong candidate’ verdict is a licence to follow up, not to conclude — the referee promotes hypotheses, it does not confirm them.

The brain-age control is a proxy, not a gold standard: it is embedding-derived (this cohort's fit: R2=0.78, MAE=2.9yr), so residual generic-aging signal can survive the adjustment and masquerade as disease-specific.
The biomarker anchor likely rests on a partial complete-case subset; with realistic p-tau217 missingness the correlation is estimated on far fewer subjects than the headline cohort.
N is small (~130); at this size AUCs are noisy and a single site's idiosyncrasy can dominate — treat effect sizes as wide-interval estimates.

The leakage test audits the embeddings with the same linear-probe family it uses for the outcome, so it bounds — but cannot fully rule out — shared confounding; this is the mechanic quantified in ‘Pretrained, Frozen, Still Leaking’ (arXiv:2606.09189 (2026)), which we cite rather than claim.
revised_caveats: Verdict is provisional and conditional on the scanner/site distribution of any replication cohort.
Brain-age adjustment uses a proxy control; residual aging signal cannot be excluded.
Any biomarker anchor is complete-case only and may not generalize.
Leakage is bounded, not eliminated — see the published batch-effect audits (‘Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings,’ arXiv:2604.14441 (2026)).
Promotion authorizes one confirmatory experiment, not adoption of the finding.

courtroom.prosecution: The claim that frozen Neuro-JEPA structural embeddings separate MCI converters from MCI non-converters does not survive scrutiny. The subject-disjoint leakage margin is only +0.04 AUC — the same head predicts the scanner nearly as well as the outcome, exactly the batch-effect mechanic reported in 'Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings' (arXiv:2604.14441 (2026)). It failed or weakened under: Site / scanner leakage (weakened). The parsimonious reading is an acquisition/aging artifact until an orthogonal molecular anchor says otherwise.
courtroom.defense: The signal separating MCI converters from MCI non-converters is consistent with real disease biology. It withstood: Age / sex adjustment, Brain-age control, Biomarker anchor, Replication split. Critically, it is anchored to a plasma p-tau217 / GFAP correlation on the complete subset (r=0.40) — molecular pathology, not pixels.
courtroom.judge_reasoning: Robustness score 88/100 places this finding at 'strong candidate'. The +0.04 AUC leakage margin is the deciding fact: the prosecution's artifact reading is the more defensible one. The finding meets the promotion floor and may proceed to the biomarker-gated biology step — as a hypothesis to test, not a conclusion to bank.

SURVIVOR.narration: The claim “MCI->AD conversion is decodable from frozen structural embeddings beyond scanner and aging.” lands at “strong candidate” (88/100). The naive effect (AUC 0.71 separating MCI converters from MCI non-converters) is real on paper, but the referee's job is to ask what else could produce it. It cleared the gauntlet's promotion floor: the effect exceeds the scanner-prediction floor on subject-disjoint splits and is worth a confirmatory follow-up — as a hypothesis, not a settled result. Where it stops being true: that the scanner/site mix in any new cohort matches this one — the leakage margin is only +0.04 AUC, so a different scanner distribution could erase the effect. That the same frozen embeddings can predict scanner/site as well as outcome is documented prior art (“Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings,” arXiv:2604.14441 (2026)); NeuroAD Referee only runs the audit and issues the verdict.

courtroom.prosecution: The claim that frozen Neuro-JEPA structural embeddings separate MCI converters from MCI non-converters does not survive scrutiny. The subject-disjoint leakage margin is only -0.13 AUC — the same head predicts the scanner nearly as well as the outcome, exactly the batch-effect mechanic reported in 'Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings' (arXiv:2604.14441 (2026)). It failed or weakened under: Age / sex adjustment (failed), Site / scanner leakage (failed), Brain-age control (failed), Biomarker anchor (failed). The parsimonious reading is an acquisition/aging artifact until an orthogonal molecular anchor says otherwise.
courtroom.defense: The signal separating MCI converters from MCI non-converters is consistent with real disease biology. It withstood: Replication split. The molecular anchor is incomplete, so the defense rests on the imaging survivors alone and asks only for follow-up, not acceptance.

The finding sits below the promotion floor; it is not carried forward to a mechanism, and would need a stronger leakage margin or molecular anchor to be worth a scientist's quarter.

KILL.narration: The claim “A structural embedding signature separates MCI converters from non-converters.” lands at “fragile” (15/100). The naive effect (AUC 0.82 separating MCI converters from MCI non-converters) is real on paper, but the referee's job is to ask what else could produce it. It sits below the promotion floor, so the honest read is that acquisition, demographics or generic aging can plausibly account for it, and it should not consume a scientist's quarter without stronger evidence. Where it stops being true: that the scanner/site mix in any new cohort matches this one — the leakage margin is only -0.13 AUC, so a different scanner distribution could erase the effect. That the same frozen embeddings can predict scanner/site as well as outcome is documented prior art (“Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings,” arXiv:2604.14441 (2026)); NeuroAD Referee only runs the audit and issues the verdict.

cohort.substrate_line: OASIS-1 + OASIS-2 structural-derived features (nWBV, eTIV, ASF, age, educ)
cohort.note: Real, vendored, no login (verified 2026-07-08). Single-scanner, so the star leakage test is a cohort/batch reframe; no plasma markers (gate NA).

SURVIVOR.label: Case A

claim.claim_text: AD vs CN diagnosis is decodable from OASIS structural-derived features (nWBV, eTIV, ASF).
claim.head: linear probe

tests.label: Age / sex adjustment
tests.question: Does the signal survive demographic covariates?
tests.detail: effect retained 92% after age/sex adjustment (AUC 0.82 -> 0.80)

tests.label: Site / scanner leakage
tests.question: Disease signal, or just which machine acquired the scan?
tests.detail: outcome clearly exceeds scanner (margin +0.25)

tests.label: Brain-age control
tests.question: More than generic aging / atrophy?
tests.detail: brain-age R2=0.57, MAE=6.4yr; effect retained 1% after brain-age-gap control

tests.label: Biomarker anchor
tests.question: Backed by molecular pathology (p-tau217 / GFAP)?
tests.detail: no plasma p-tau217 / GFAP coverage at usable n — cannot anchor (route to ADNI/EPAD)

tests.label: Replication split
tests.question: Reproduces on a held-out site / cohort?
tests.detail: held-out cohort AUC=0.79 (train 0.85); n_test=98

SURVIVOR.gate_note: Not promoted: the biomarker anchor is untestable on this cohort (no plasma p-tau217/GFAP). Refused pending molecular confirmation — the single next step is the anchor test below, in a cohort that ships the panel.
next_experiment: Carry this exact probe to ADNI-3 or EPAD (both ship plasma p-tau217 + GFAP) and run the anchor the OASIS cohort cannot.
Promote only if the probe score tracks p-tau217 (95% CI lower bound > 0); if the correlation includes zero, the structural signal is not molecularly anchored.
falsification: If the AD/CN separation is fully explained by brain age (it already loses most of its effect under the brain-age control here), it is atrophy, not AD-specific.
caveats: Biomarker anchor unavailable (no plasma p-tau217/GFAP coverage): claim cannot be promoted until molecularly anchored.
Completeness: 1/5 gauntlet tests could not run; score is renormalized over the tests that did.
'Partially robust' is not 'robust' — survives some but not all challenges.
Verdict is provisional and conditional on the scanner/site distribution of any replication cohort.
Brain-age adjustment uses a proxy control; residual aging signal cannot be excluded.
Any biomarker anchor is complete-case only and may not generalize.
Leakage is bounded, not eliminated — see the published batch-effect audits (‘Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings,’ arXiv:2604.14441 (2026)).
‘Partially robust’ is not ‘robust’. The score renormalizes over the tests that ran, so a middling verdict can hide a dimension that quietly failed — read the per-test results, not just the headline.

The brain-age control is a proxy, not a gold standard: it is embedding-derived (this cohort's fit: R2=0.57, MAE=6.3yr), so residual generic-aging signal can survive the adjustment and masquerade as disease-specific.
There is no usable plasma p-tau217 / GFAP anchor here, so the molecular gate is unmet — the finding rests on imaging alone.
Confirm the effective sample size per split — subject-disjoint cross-validation on a modest cohort leaves each fold thin.

The leakage test audits the embeddings with the same linear-probe family it uses for the outcome, so it bounds — but cannot fully rule out — shared confounding; this is the mechanic quantified in ‘Pretrained, Frozen, Still Leaking’ (arXiv:2606.09189 (2026)), which we cite rather than claim.

Leakage is bounded, not eliminated — see the published batch-effect audits (‘Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings,’ arXiv:2604.14441 (2026)).

courtroom.prosecution: The claim that OASIS structural-derived features (weight-free feeder) separate AD (CDR>=1) from CN (CDR=0) does not survive scrutiny. Even granting a +0.25 AUC leakage margin, that headroom is thin enough that acquisition confounds could account for much of the apparent effect. It failed or weakened under: Brain-age control (failed). The parsimonious reading is an acquisition/aging artifact until an orthogonal molecular anchor says otherwise.
courtroom.defense: The signal separating AD (CDR>=1) from CN (CDR=0) is consistent with real disease biology. It withstood: Age / sex adjustment, Site / scanner leakage, Replication split. The outcome exceeds the scanner-prediction floor by +0.25 AUC on subject-disjoint splits — the effect is not merely which machine acquired the scan. The molecular anchor is incomplete, so the defense rests on the imaging survivors alone and asks only for follow-up, not acceptance.
courtroom.judge_reasoning: Robustness score 69/100 places this finding at 'partially robust'. 1 dimension(s) could not be run and were excluded from the denominator — the verdict reflects only the evidence actually gathered. The +0.25 AUC leakage margin clears the acquisition confound, which is why the defense retains standing. The finding sits below the promotion floor; it is not carried forward to a mechanism, and would need a stronger leakage margin or molecular anchor to be worth a scientist's quarter.

SURVIVOR.narration: The claim “AD vs CN diagnosis is decodable from OASIS structural-derived features.” lands at “partially robust” (69/100). The naive effect (AUC 0.82 separating AD (CDR>=1) from CN (CDR=0)) is real on paper, but the referee's job is to ask what else could produce it. It sits below the promotion floor, so the honest read is that acquisition, demographics or generic aging can plausibly account for it, and it should not consume a scientist's quarter without stronger evidence. Where it stops being true: that the signal is more than accelerated brain-age; a chunk of it co-varies with the brain-age control, so in a cohort matched on brain-age gap the separation would shrink. That the same frozen embeddings can predict scanner/site as well as outcome is documented prior art (“Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings,” arXiv:2604.14441 (2026)); NeuroAD Referee only runs the audit and issues the verdict.

real_evidence.message: The structural embedding predicts the scanner (field strength) at AUC 0.891 in 3984 healthy subjects with NO disease — this is the batch effect the referee gates against, on REAL data (not synthetic). Prior art: 'Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings' (arXiv:2604.14441 (2026)).

discovery.note: Discovered 3 cluster(s) via kmeans (silhouette=0.063). 1 promotable phenotype(s); 2 flagged as likely acquisition/age/sex artifact(s). Recovery vs planted ground truth: ARI=1.0, AMI=1.0.
